Notes: Always preserve Exact primary key for reconciliation and re-run safety.
Exact's business-facing subscription number. Odoo auto-generates 'name' (Order Reference), so this goes to a custom field.
Subscription description. Odoo sale.order has no 'description' field — 'name' is the auto-generated order reference, 'note' is terms & conditions, 'internal_note' is internal. Safest as custom.
Subscriber account. Requires lookup: Exact Account GUID → Odoo res.partner.
Contact person GUID on subscriber side. Odoo has no direct contact-person field on sale.order. Consider resolving to a child res.partner if contact data is migrated.
Denormalized display field. Value derivable from OrderedByContactPerson relation.
Denormalized display field. Value derivable from OrderedBy → res.partner.name.
Invoice-to account. Requires same Account → res.partner lookup.
Contact person on invoice-to side. No native sale.order equivalent for contact-level granularity.
Denormalized display field. Value derivable from InvoiceToContactPerson relation.
Denormalized display field. Value derivable from InvoiceTo → res.partner.name.
Direct mapping. Exact DateTime → Odoo date (strip time component).
Direct mapping. Odoo: 'If set in advance, the subscription will be set to renew 1 month before the date and will be closed on the date set in this field.'
No native cancellation-date field in Odoo. Odoo tracks closure via subscription_state and close_reason_id but not the cancellation date itself.
Date invoicing begins. Odoo's next_invoice_date is the rolling next date, not the original start. Safest as custom.

Date up to which invoicing has been completed. Odoo has last_invoice_date (date of last invoice), which is close but semantically different — Exact means 'period covered', Odoo means 'date generated'. Custom avoids ambiguity.
Direct mapping.
Direct mapping.
Map via SubscriptionTypes → sale.subscription.plan migration. Requires that SubscriptionTypes are migrated first.
Denormalized display field. Value derivable from SubscriptionType → sale.subscription.plan.
Denormalized display field. Value derivable from SubscriptionType → sale.subscription.plan.name.
Day-of-month (or weekday) for invoice generation. Odoo's sale.subscription.plan has billing_first_day (boolean: align to 1st), which is much simpler. No equivalent for arbitrary day selection.
Exact stores currency as ISO code string. Resolve to res.currency record by name/code.
Direct mapping. Both are customer-side purchase order references.
Requires mapping Exact payment condition codes → Odoo payment terms.
Denormalized display field. Value derivable from PaymentCondition → account.payment.term.name.
Map via SubscriptionReasonCodes → sale.order.close.reason migration (see ReasonCodes sheet).
Denormalized display field. Value derivable from ReasonCancelled → sale.order.close.reason.
Denormalized display field. Value derivable from ReasonCancelled → sale.order.close.reason.name.
Exact classification concept. No Odoo equivalent. Odoo has tag_ids (many2many → crm.tag) which could be used alternatively, but a custom field preserves the original GUID for traceability.
Denormalized display field. Value derivable from Classification relation.
Denormalized display field. Value derivable from Classification relation.
Requires user mapping: Exact user GUID → Odoo res.users. May default to admin if user mapping is not in scope.
Denormalized display field. Value derivable from Creator → res.users.name.
Same user mapping requirement as Creator.
Denormalized display field. Value derivable from Modifier → res.users.name.
Exact Division ≈ Odoo Company. Requires division-to-company mapping.

Best fit for additional subscription info. Odoo 'internal_note' is internal-facing free text. Wrap plain text in

tags if needed.
Exact-specific: blocks time cost entry. No Odoo equivalent.
Exact-specific: indicates if subscription was printed. No Odoo equivalent.
Reference to Exact Project. Odoo sale.order does not have a native project link in the subscription context. If project module is installed, a custom many2one → project.project could be used instead.
Structural: lines are migrated separately per the SubLines sheet.
Structural reference. No Odoo equivalent. Could become a separate custom model or JSON blob if needed.
Structural reference. No Odoo equivalent. Same approach as above.
Always set to True for migrated subscription records.
Derive from Exact lifecycle: if CancellationDate is set → '6_churn' or '5_renewed'; if EndDate < today → '6_churn'; if StartDate ≤ today ≤ EndDate → '3_progress'; etc. Requires mapping logic.
Auto-generated by Odoo on creation (e.g. 'S00001'). Could optionally embed Exact Number via origin field or let Odoo sequence handle it.
Odoo sales order state (draft/sale/cancel). Migrated subscriptions should typically be set to 'sale'.
